metadata.title:
MR.. SWINCLER DEFENDS


The HANCMAN BRIDGE

pages.raw_text:
expended in grading streets, or in clear
ing roadways. on in policing any part
Of that section of the city beyond
Hangman creek, nor have they had one
electric light. In other words. they
have paid large revenue to the city
and have had TO make their own road
ways and light themselves home with
a lantern at night. and since the bridge
has been declared unsafe for teams,
they have had to walk across it, and
are still doing so. 1t is true they are
running great risk in walking across
the old structure. but they prefer TO
do this rather than 90 down into the
deep ravine and up the long, toilsome
hill ON the other side.


Under these circumstances my neigh
bors and myself feel that we are not
handing that the city and coanty,
acting together, build a new bridge N
the place of the old bridge, whch has
been used for many years, and Was
built entirely. as before stated, bf pri
vate subscription.


Would you ask the people of this
district to build and repair bridge in
definitely? Would it be justice To
them?


Furthermore, this bridge servet not
only the people who live withi1 the
city limits beyond Hangman freeli
chasm. but also hundreds of otheitpeoi
pie who live in the county, butt out
side the city limits, and the Ct Pmty
commissioners are well aware Of the
justice in the demand of the cit Eens,
who live in the western part d the
county, for a high bridge. because it
will save them long. toilsome route,
every time they visit the city, and Ger
tainly the city of Spokane wants TO
make it as easy as may be for the
country people to come into the city
to do their marketing and trading.


Yours respectfully,


NJ. NJ. SWINGLER.
